04 February 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • U.S. Forces Strike ISIS Targets in Syria as Partners Sustain Pressure CENTCOM 04 Feb 2026 -- U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) conducted five strikes against multiple ISIS targets across Syria, Jan. 27 - Feb. 2, as partner forces continue to apply military pressure to ensure the enduring defeat of the terrorist network.

  • US Confirms Deployment of Small Team of Troops to Nigeria — Reports Sputnik 04 Feb 2026 -- The United States has sent a small group of soldiers to Nigeria, marking the first confirmed presence of US forces on the ground since airstrikes carried out in December, Reuters reported.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • U.S. still seeks to take over Greenland, despite resumption of diplomatic talks: Greenlandic PM People's Daily 04 Feb 2026 -- Despite the resumption of diplomatic talks with the U.S. government, Washington's desire to take over Greenland remains, the island's Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen said in an interview with Danish Broadcasting Corporation (DR) published Tuesday.

  • Readout of Under Secretary for War for Policy Elbridge Colby Visit to the Republic of Korea and Japan DoD 04 Feb 2026
  • Marines Prepare for Exercise Cold Response in Norway DoD News 04 Feb 2026 -- A technical assistance and advisory team assigned to Blount Island Command, Marine Corps Support Facility Blount Island in Jacksonville, Florida, issued thousands of items to II Marine Expeditionary Force during a two-week cave draw in late January, marking a key step in unit preparations for Exercise Cold Response 26.
  • National Guard Field Artillery Troops Get Acquainted With Cold Weather DoD News 04 Feb 2026 -- Approximately 200 soldiers assigned to the Wisconsin National Guard's 1st Battalion, 120th Field Artillery Regiment, went to Fort McCoy, Wisconsin, Jan. 21, to not only complete important individual soldier tasks but also to train on M777 and M119 howitzers in cold weather.
  • Coast Guard takes delivery of 18th mission-ready HC-130J surveillance aircraft US Coast Guard 04 Feb 2026 -- The Coast Guard is completing final delivery activities this week for its 18th fully missionized HC-130J long range surveillance aircraft, designated CGNR 2018, at L3Harris Integrated Mission Systems in Waco, Texas. The aircraft will support the transition of Air Station Sacramento, California, from C-27J aircraft to HC-130Js.
  • Exercise Cutlass Express 2026 to Enhance Maritime Security in East Africa US Navy Press Release 04 Feb 2026 -- Exercise Cutlass Express 2026 sponsored by U.S. Africa Command (AFRICOM) and enabled by U.S. Sixth Fleet kicks off in Port Louis, Mauritius Feb. 2-12.
  • PCU John F. Kennedy (CVN 79) Completes Builder's Sea Trials US Navy Press Release 04 Feb 2026 -- Completion of Builder's Trials marks a significant shipbuilding milestone for the future John F. Kennedy, the second ship in the Gerald R. Ford-class of aircraft carriers designed to improve survivability, increase lethality, and drive down total ownership cost over their expected 50-year lifespan.
  • CAT 4361, U.S Southern Command Humanitarian Assistance Program, and Guatemala Partner to Provide Medical Care in Puerto San Jose SOUTHCOM 04 Feb 2026 -- Civil Affairs Team 4361, assigned to Army Forces Battalion, Joint Task Force-Bravo, alongside U.S. Southern Command's Humanitarian Assistance Program, partnered with the Guatemalan Department of Health of Escuintla to bring vital medical services and approximately $12,000 worth of medications and supplies to communities in Puerto San Jose, Guatemala, Jan. 29-30, 2026.
  • Matryoshka Satellites, Robotic Arms: Former US Space Force Commander Warns Of Russian, Chinese Threats RFE/RL 04 Feb 2026 -- When a Russian "nesting-doll" satellite maneuvered into close proximity to a US satellite last June, it was the latest move in a dangerous game of cat-and-mouse in space.

  • UN chief warns of 'grave moment' as final US-Russia nuclear arms treaty expires UN News 04 Feb 2026 -- UN Secretary-General António Guterres has warned that the expiration of the New START treaty represents a "grave moment" for international peace and security, as binding limits on US and Russian strategic nuclear weapons fall away amid heightened global tensions.
  • Statement by the Secretary-General on the occasion of the expiration of the Treaty on Measures for the Further Reduction and Limitation of Strategic Offensive Arms (New START) Treaty on 5 February 2026 UN Secretary-General 05 Feb 2026
  • Statement of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Russia Concerning the Expiration of the Russia-US New START Treaty Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 04 Feb 2026
  • US's Lack of Response to Russia's New START Proposals Regrettable - Foreign Ministry Sputnik 04 Feb 2026 -- Washington's approach of ignoring Russia's ideas on the New Strategic Arms Reduction Treaty (New START) is regrettable, the Russian Foreign Ministry said on Wednesday.

  • Eastern Sentry Joint Planning Team deploys to Bulgaria strengthen collaboration on NATOs Eastern Flank NATO Allied Air Command 04 Feb 2026 -- A NATO Joint Planning Team (JPT), composed of personnel from Allied Air Command (AIRCOM), Joint Force Command Naples (JFC Naples) and the Combined Air Operations Centre (CAOC) Torrejón, deployed to Sofia to further develop the Bulgarian contribution to Eastern Sentry.
  • Exercise Arctic Dolphin 26 strengthens Allied anti-submarine warfare training in Norway NATO Allied Maritime Command 04 Feb 2026 -- Exercise Arctic Dolphin 26 is underway in Vestlandet, Norway, bringing together Norwegian and Allied naval forces for two weeks of intensive training under Arctic conditions. Running from 2 to 13 February 26, the exercise focuses on anti-submarine warfare (ASW) while also certifying new submarine commanders in one of NATO's most demanding maritime environments.

  • Minister of Defence of Russian Federation Andrei Belousov set task to ensure continuous production and supply of special-demand samples of weaponry, military and special hardware pieces Russia MoD 04 Feb 2026 -- The Minister of Defence of the Russian Federation, Andrei Belousov held a meeting on the production and supply of specially demanded weapons, military and special hardware.

  • Commanders at all levels are urged to immediately verify all Starlink terminals used for defense Ukraine MOD 04 Feb 2026
  • Ministry of Defence of Ukraine and RTX discuss accelerating Patriot missile deliveries Ukraine MOD 04 Feb 2026
  • Ukraine to gain access to €140 million in EU credit financing for dual-use technology development Ukraine MOD 04 Feb 2026

  • Australia to Pawn Off Historic Defense Sites to Fund US-Directed AUKUS Militarization Sputnik 04 Feb 2026 -- Australia, which has been so eager to boost its defense spending budget, is hoping to unlock up to $1.8 billion to allow the Australian Defense Force to tackle critical capability gaps, reports ABC News.
  • Delivering the Future Defence Estate Australia DOD 04 Feb 2026 -- The Albanese Government is undertaking the most significant reform to the Defence estate ever to ensure it is fit-for-purpose - providing the ADF with the facilities and capabilities it needs to keep Australians safe.

  • "This Year Marks the Foundational Year of OPCON Transition—We Will Fulfill Our Mission Without Fail" ROK MND 04 Feb 2026 -- Minister Ahn Gyu-back chairs the Operational Control Transfer Promotion Evaluation Meeting.
  • ROK-US Pledge Close Coordination on Nuclear Submarine Construction and OPCON Transition ROK MND 04 Feb 2026 -- Minister of National Defense Ahn Gyu-back and US Under Secretary of Defense for policy Elbridge Colby of the US Department of War pledged cooperation on January 26 regarding the ROK's construction of nuclear submarines, the transfer of wartime operational control (OPCON), and the pursuit of North Korea policy aimed at peaceful coexistence on the Korean Peninsula.
  • "Seeking Solutions to Defense Challenges through the Collective Wisdom of the Civilian, Government, and Military Sectors" ROK MND 04 Feb 2026 -- The Civilian-Government-Military Joint Special Advisory Committee for Overcoming Internal Crisis and Designing Future Defense (Joint Committee), which discussed core reform issues for our military and proposed policy measures to the Minister of National Defense, officially concluded its activities on January 22.
  • Minister Ahn Calls for Military Intelligence and Investigative Institutions to Reform Outdated Structures to Rebuild Public Trus... ROK MND 04 Feb 2026 -- Defense Minister Ahn Gyu-back emphasized on January 21 that the three military intelligence and investigative institutions—the Defense Counterintelligence Command (DCC), the Defense Intelligence Command (DIC), and the Criminal Investigation Command (CIC)—must "resolutely overhaul their outdated structures with a commitment to fundamental transformation, and rebuild public trust through ceaseless innovation."
  • "Restoring Public Trust Marks the Starting Point of Defense Reform" ROK MND 04 Feb 2026 -- Minister of National Defense Ahn Gyu-back unequivocally stated to the ROK Army, Navy, Air Force, and Marine Corps on January 19 that "a military lacking the trust of the people is merely a castle built on sand," emphasizing that restoring public trust is the starting point of defense reform.

  • Under Leadership Directives, Minister of Defense Meets with Amir of Qatar SPA 04 Feb 2026 -- Under the directives of the leadership, Minister of Defense Prince Khalid bin Salman bin Abdulaziz met with Amir of the State of Qatar Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani today at the Amiri Diwan in Doha
  • US approves $3bn deal for sale of F-15 sustainment equipment to Saudi Arabia Press TV 04 Feb 2026 -- The United States has approved a potential $3 billion deal for sale of F-15 fighter jet sustainment equipment to Saudi Arabia, furthering its controversial arms race in the Persian Gulf.

  • INDIA - KYRGYZSTAN JOINT SPECIAL FORCES EXERCISE KHANJAR COMMENCES IN ASSAM India PIB 04 Feb 2026 -- The 13th edition of the India-Kyrgyzstan Joint Special Forces Exercise KHANJAR, scheduled from 04 to 17 February 2026, has commenced in Missamari, Assam

  • EU-Armenia: Joint Press Release on the 3rd Political and Security Dialogue European External Action Service (EEAS) 04 Feb 2026 -- The 3rd high-level EU-Armenia Political and Security Dialogue took place on 3 February in Yerevan. It reaffirmed the strategic ambition of the EU-Armenia partnership and shared commitment to advance cooperation and alignment in foreign, security and defence policy. It also underlined the importance of continued, structured exchanges on political and security matters as an important pillar of the EU-Armenia Comprehensive and Enhanced Partnership Agreement (CEPA).

  • First Steel Cutting Ceremony for PPA No 8 OCCAR 04 Feb 2026 -- The First Steel Cutting ceremony for the eighth Multipurpose Patrol Ship (PPA 8) took place at Fincantieri's integrated shipyard in Riva Trigoso (Genoa).

  • A look at the Defence news 26 January - 1 February Netherlands Ministry of Defence 04 Feb 2026

  • Senate must bankroll AFP, Coast Guard amid WPS tensions -Pangilinan Philippine News Agency 04 Feb 2026 -- Senator Francis Pangilinan urged the Senate to sustain budget support for the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) and the Philippine Coast Guard (PCG), saying stronger funding is essential to enforcing the country's maritime rights in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) even as lawmakers weighed differing views on how to manage tensions with China.

  • Romanian-American joint training activities conducted in Smârdan Training Range Romania - Ministry of National Defence 04 Feb 2026 -- Soldiers of the 284th Armor Battalion "Cuza Vodă" and American soldiers who participated with five M1A2 SEPv2 Abrams Tanks conducted joint training activities between January 29th and February 4th, at the Smârdan Training Range, in Galați County.

  • Russia Urges International Community to Curb Arms Flow From Ukraine to Africa - Russia's UN Envoy Sputnik 04 Feb 2026 -- Russia calls on the international community to prevent the trafficking of arms and Starlink terminals from Ukraine to militants in African countries, Russian Permanent Representative to the United Nations Vassily Nebenzia said on Wednesday.

  • Ranking Member Shaheen, Senator Curtis Welcome Critical Minerals Ministerial and Urge Colleagues to Back Legislation to Secure Critical Minerals Supply Chains US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 04 Feb 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, and John Curtis (R-UT), member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, released the following statement as the United States hosts a Critical Minerals Ministerial.
  • Risch on Critical Minerals Agreements with EU, Mexico, and Japan US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 04 Feb 2026 -- U.S. Senator Jim Risch (R-Idaho), chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, today released the statement below regarding the critical minerals agreements recently signed between the U.S. and Mexico, the European Union, and Japan.
  • Vice President JD Vance, Secretary of State Marco Rubio, Japanese State Minister for Foreign Affairs Horii Iwao, Special Assistant to the President of the United States and Senior Director for Global Supply Chains David Copley, and Under Secretary of State for Economic Affairs Jacob Helberg at the Opening Remarks of the Critical Minerals Ministerial US Dept. of State 04 Feb 2026
  • 2026 Critical Minerals Ministerial US Dept. of State 04 Feb 2026 -- Today the United States, together with our partners and allies, has set out to reshape the global market for critical minerals and rare earths. Secretary of State Marco Rubio, joined by Vice President JD Vance, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, Interior Secretary Doug Burgum, Energy Secretary Chris Wright, and U.S. Trade Representative Ambassador Jamieson Greer, hosted representatives of 54 countries and the European Commission, including 43 foreign and other ministers, at the 2026 Critical Minerals Ministerial.
  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio at a Press Availability US Dept. of State 04 Feb 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Costa Rican President-Elect Fernández US Dept. of State 04 Feb 2026
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Uzbekistan Foreign Minister Saidov US Dept. of State 04 Feb 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Bolivian Foreign Minister Aramayo US Dept. of State 04 Feb 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Democratic Republic of the Congo President Tshisekedi US Dept. of State 04 Feb 2026
  • Shaheen, Risch Meet with DRC President Félix Tshisekedi US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 04 Feb 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH) and Jim Risch (R-ID), Ranking Member and Chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, released the statement below following a meeting with President Félix Tshisekedi of the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC).
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Kazakhstan Foreign Minister Kosherbayev US Dept. of State 04 Feb 2026

  • Activity of Unit 869 to Prevent the Rehabilitation of the Hezbollah Terrorist Organization Israel Defense Forces 04 Feb 2026

  • Israel to shut water, electricity at UNRWA facilities in occupied territories Press TV 04 Feb 2026 -- Israel will begin cutting off water and electricity to United Nations Relief and Works Agency (UNRWA) facilities in the occupied Palestinian territories, a top Israeli minister announced on Wednesday.
  • At least 20 killed in heavy Israeli bombing of displacement tents in Gaza Press TV 04 Feb 2026 -- Israeli warplanes have carried out heavy strikes on tents housing displaced Palestinians in Khan Yunis, killing civilians and overwhelming local hospitals, in clear violation of the ceasefire agreement with the Palestinian resistance movement Hamas.

  • Israel: UN experts urge withdrawal of death penalty bill OHCHR 04 Feb 2026 -- UN experts* today urged Israel to withdraw a bill proposing the mandatory death penalty for terrorist acts, which would violate the right to life and discriminate against Palestinians in the occupied Palestinian territory.
  • 'We are dying': Gaza's cancer patients plead for a way out UN News 04 Feb 2026 -- As World Cancer Day is marked on Wednesday, thousands of patients in Gaza face worsening illness, untreated pain and closed crossings - despite the limited opening of the vital route through Rafah this week.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, Syria, Yemen UNOCHA 04 Feb 2026

  • Russia must end reprisals and intimidation of ICC Prosecutor and judges: UN Special Rapporteurs OHCHR 04 Feb 2026 -- UN human rights experts* today condemned the Moscow City Court's conviction of the Prosecutor of the International Criminal Court (ICC) and eight sitting ICC judges, calling it a flagrant violation of international law.

  • Exclusion zones to be set for WPS drills Philippine News Agency 04 Feb 2026 -- Once live-fire exercises are finalized, exclusion zones will be set in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) where the Philippines and its "treaty ally" are conducting a series of naval and air drills that began on Jan. 21 and run until March 31.

  • Pakistan deploys helicopters, drones to retake town from insurgents as scores killed Press TV 04 Feb 2026 -- Pakistan's security forces have deployed helicopters and drones to regain control of a town in the country's largest province after separatist insurgents seized key installations, resulting in a death toll of 58 in the restive region.
  • What's Behind The Unprecedented Attacks In Pakistan's Balochistan? RFE/RL 04 Feb 2026 -- Pakistan's vast and mineral-rich province of Balochistan has witnessed the largest-ever coordinated attacks by separatist militants, who are waging an increasingly potent insurgency against Islamabad.

  • South Sudan: UN forced to suspend food aid after 'unacceptable' attacks on convoy UN News 04 Feb 2026 -- South Sudan is buckling under a fresh wave of violence and displacement, after attacks and looting halted a major UN food convoy in restive Upper Nile state and clashes continue to spread in neighbouring Jonglei.
  • WFP suspends activities in Baliet County and urgently calls for protection of humanitarian space in South Sudan WFP 04 Feb 2026 -- The United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) strongly condemns the recent attacks on its river convoy in Upper Nile State over the weekend. WFP has suspended all activities in Baliet County until the safety and security of its staff, partners and contractors is assured and the Government of South Sudan has taken immediate measures to recover the stolen commodities.

  • Sudan Humanitarian Fund Call to Action Event US Dept. of State 04 Feb 2026 -- The scale and severity of the humanitarian crisis in Sudan requires an urgent, sustained response.
  • Foreign Secretary slams collective global failure to protect Sudan's women and girls UK FCDO 04 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Secretary visited Adré on the Sudanese border in Chad and called for urgent global action to secure a ceasefire.

News Reports

  • Ryan Wesley Routh Sentenced to Life in Prison for Attempted Assassination of President Donald J. Trump and Assault of a Federal Law Enforcement Officer US Dept. of Justice 04 Feb 2026 -- Today, Ryan Wesley Routh, 59, was sentenced to life plus 84 months in federal prison for the attempted assassination of then-presidential candidate Donald J. Trump and related violent and firearms offenses. U.S. District Judge Aileen M. Cannon for the Southern District of Florida imposed the sentence following Routh's conviction by a federal jury on all five counts charged in the indictment.

  • Intensifying ISIL threat highlights need to step up counter-terrorism measures UN News 04 Feb 2026 -- The terrorist group ISIL continues to adapt and demonstrate resilience despite sustained counter-terrorism efforts, a senior UN official told the Security Council on Wednesday.
  • Terrorists' Use of Emerging Technologies Poses Evolving Threat to International Peace, Stability, Acting UN Counter-Terrorism Chief Warns Security Council UN Security Council 04 Feb 2026 -- Islamic State in Iraq and the Levant (ISIL/Da'esh) and its affiliated terrorist groups continue to adapt and pose a threat to international peace and security, speakers in the Security Council warned today, citing increased use of virtual assets, including cryptocurrencies, as well as cybertools, unmanned aircraft systems and advanced artificial intelligence (AI).
  • We will continue to disrupt the activities of Daesh's networks: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 04 Feb 2026 -- Statement by Chris Elmore MP, Minister for Multilateral, Human Rights, Latin America and the Caribbean, at the UN Security Council meeting on counter-terrorism.

  • The General Secretariat of the OIC Condemns the Recent Terrorist Attack on the Hamani Diouri International Airport in Niamey Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 04 Feb 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has condemned in the strongest terms the recent terrorist attack by an armed group on the Hamani Diouri International Airport in Niamey

  • Videoconference with President of China Xi Jinping President of Russia 04 Feb 2026 -- Vladimir Putin held talks with President of People's Republic of China Xi Jinping via videoconference.
  • Commentary by Presidential Aide Yury Ushakov following Vladimir Putin's videoconference with President of China Xi Jinping President of Russia 04 Feb 2026
  • President Xi Jinping Has Virtual Meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin PRC MofA 04 Feb 2026 -- On the afternoon of February 4, President Xi Jinping had a virtual meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing at the Great Hall of the People.
  • Xi calls on China, Russia to grow ties, work for global strategic stability PRC MND 04 Feb 2026 -- China and Russia should increase high-level exchanges and strengthen pragmatic cooperation in various fields, Chinese President Xi Jinping said in a virtual meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin on Wednesday.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on February 4, 2026 PRC MofA 04 Feb 2026
  • President Xi Jinping Speaks with U.S. President Donald J. Trump on the Phone PRC MofA 04 Feb 2026 -- On the evening of February 4, 2026, President Xi Jinping spoke with U.S. President Donald J. Trump on the phone.
  • Trump Says Discussed Russia-Ukraine, Taiwan, Iran During 'Excellent' Call With Xi Sputnik 04 Feb 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said he held an "excellent conversation" with Chinese President Xi Jinping on Wednesday, discussing issues pertaining to the Russia-Ukraine conflict, Taiwan and Iran.
  • Xi says China consistently attaches great importance to ties with Vietnam PRC MND 04 Feb 2026 -- China will consistently attach great importance to the development of China-Vietnam relations, said Xi Jinping, general secretary of the Communist Party of China (CPC) Central Committee and Chinese president, on Wednesday.
  • From Classrooms To TikTok, China's Soft Power Push Expands In The Balkans RFE/RL 04 Feb 2026 -- As China's big-ticket investments draw scrutiny across Europe, Beijing is turning to a quieter form of influence in the Balkans: scholarships, university exchanges, and young students who share their lives in China with audiences back home.
  • China's Ministry of Public Security releases cases of illegal drone flights bypassing altitude restrictions near military airfields and airspace Global Times 04 Feb 2026 -- China's Ministry of Public Security (MPS) on Wednesday released eight typical cases involving illegal drone flights, drawn from recent years, as unauthorized drone operations have increasingly disrupted airspace order and posed risks to civil aviation safety, according to the ministry's official WeChat account.
  • China intensifies corruption crackdown: top procuratorate People's Daily 04 Feb 2026 -- China has stepped up its crackdown on duty-related crimes over the past year, particularly those involving bribery in key areas and sectors vital to people's livelihoods, the Supreme People's Procuratorate (SPP) announced on Tuesday.

  • TPP defense act placed on its priority list for new legislative session CNA 04 Feb 2026 -- The Taiwan People's Party (TPP) on Wednesday announced its 21 "priority" legislative bills, including its own version of a national defense special act, which caps arms spending at NT$400 billion (US$12.66 billion) through 2033, and requires that funding be allotted on a yearly basis.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 04 Feb 2026
  • Think tank forum co-hosted by CPC, KMT held in Beijing; dialogue channel may pave way for higher-level political dialogue: expert Global Times 04 Feb 2026 -- A think tank forum co-hosted by research institutes affiliated with the Communist Party of China (CPC) and the Chinese Kuomintang Party (KMT) kicked off in Beijing on Tuesday, marking the revival of the forum mechanism after a nine-year hiatus amid prolonged cross-Straits tensions since the secessionist Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) took office.
  • CPC, KMT think tank forum proposes common opinions on promoting cross-Strait cooperation People's Daily 04 Feb 2026 -- A series of common opinions on promoting cross-Strait exchanges and cooperation was proposed at a think tank forum co-hosted by research institutes affiliated with the Communist Party of China (CPC) and the Chinese Kuomintang (KMT) party, which was held in Beijing on Tues

  • FM holds talks in Washington on tariff agreement, North Korea KOREA.net 04 Feb 2026 -- Minister of Foreign Affairs Cho Hyun on Feb. 3 spoke to U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio in Washington on major issues of common interest like the implementation of a joint fact sheet on bilateral trade and security agreements and North Korea.

  • Iran, US to hold nuclear talks in Muscat on Friday: FM Press TV 04 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has announced that nuclear negotiations between Iran and the United States are scheduled to take place in the Omani capital Muscat on Friday morning.
  • The Key Sticking Points To A US-Iran Deal Aimed At Averting War RFE/RL 04 Feb 2026 -- White House special envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi will meet on February 6 in a last-ditch attempt to strike a deal aimed at averting war.
  • US Willing to Engage Directly With Iranian Counterparts If Such Opportunity Exists - Rubio Sputnik 04 Feb 2026 -- The United States is ready to engage in direct conversation with Iran if there is such an opportunity, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Wednesday.
  • US Accepts Iran's Request to Move Talks From Turkiye - Reports Sputnik 04 Feb 2026 -- The administration of US President Donald Trump has accepted Tehran's request to move talks on the Iranian nuclear program from Turkiye to Oman, Axios journalist Barak Ravid reported on Wednesday, citing a source.
  • Iran intelligence minister warns West will face consequences for IRGC designation Press TV 04 Feb 2026 -- Iran's Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib has warned that Western countries will bear the consequences of designating the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization.
  • Araghchi reacts to Israeli regime's efforts to drag US into war with Iran IRNA 04 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has reacted to the Israeli regime's efforts to drag the United States into war with Iran.
  • Oman to broker fresh round of Iran-US talks on February 6: Reports Press TV 04 Feb 2026 -- Iran will hold indirect nuclear negotiations with the United States on February 6 in Oman's capital, Muscat, Iranian media reported on Wednesday.
  • Israel to Support Attack on Iran If It Fails to Reach Broad Agreement With US - Reports Sputnik 04 Feb 2026 -- Israel will support an attack on Iran if the agreement between Tehran and Washington does not include measures to limit ballistic missile production and Iran's support for proxy groups in the region, the Financial Times newspaper reported, citing a source.
  • Freedom Online Coalition Joint Statement on Internet Shutdowns in the Islamic Republic of Iran Canada 04 Feb 2026
  • U.S.-Iran drone encounter clouds prospects for talks People's Daily 04 Feb 2026 -- A drone belonging to Iran's armed forces completed a "surveillance mission" in international waters Tuesday, Iranian media reported, shortly after the U.S. military said it had shot down an Iranian aircraft that "aggressively approached" an aircraft carrier.
  • Foreign Ministry spokesperson elaborates on upcoming talks IRNA 04 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei says planning for the negotiations has been made for the coming days, and consultations to determine the venue for the talks are currently underway while information will be shared as soon as it is finalized.

  • Comment by Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova on the killing of Saif al-Islam Gadhafi in Libya Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 04 Feb 2026
  • Libya's Prosecutor General's Office Confirms Gaddafi's Son Killed by Gunfire Sputnik 04 Feb 2026 -- Libya's prosecutor general's office has confirmed that Libyan politician Saif al-Islam Gaddafi, the son of former leader Muammar Gaddafi, was killed by gunshot wounds.
  • Son of Libya longtime ruler Qaddafi killed IRNA 04 Feb 2026 -- Seif Al-Islam Qaddafi, the son of Libya's late longtime ruler, has been killed, reports said on Tuesday.
  • Son of former Libyan ruler Gaddafi killed in 'home attack': Aides Press TV 04 Feb 2026 -- Saif al-Islam Gaddafi, the son of former Libyan ruler Muammar Gaddafi, has been killed in Libya, according to his political team and local media.

  • JOINT DECLARATION ON ESTABLISHING STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIP BETWEEN THE ISLAMIC REPUBLIC OF PAKISTAN AND THЕ REPUBLIC OF KAZAKHSTAN Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 04 Feb 2026 -- At the invitation of the Prime Minister of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan H.E. Mr. Muhammad Shehbaz Sharif, the President of the Republic of Kazakhstan H.E. Mr. Kassym-Jomart Tokayev paid a State Visit to the Islamic Republic of Pakistan on 3-4 February 2026.

  • NUCLEAR SAFETY AND SECURITY India PIB 04 Feb 2026 -- IN-SPACe does not have registration process for start-ups as it will be a duplication of DPIIT's role. However, 399 number of space technology start-ups (includes satellites, development of launch vehicles etc.) registered with DPIIT as per Startups India portal.
  • STRENGTHENING NATIONAL SPACE INFRASTRUCTURE India PIB 04 Feb 2026 -- Progress made in strengthening national launch and space infrastructure during 2025:
  • NUCLEAR SAFETY AND SECURITY India PIB 04 Feb 2026 -- IN-SPACe does not have registration process for start-ups as it will be a duplication of DPIIT's role
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: DEVELOPMENT OF SMALL MODULAR REACTOR India PIB 04 Feb 2026 -- Nuclear energy is envisaged a clean and reliable energy source
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: NUCLEAR COOPERATION WITH RUSSIA India PIB 04 Feb 2026 -- Regular discussions are on between the Department of Atomic Energy (DAE) and ROSATOM to strengthen cooperation in the field of nuclear energy, including in the area of large capacity Nuclear Power Plants and Small Modular Reactors (SMRs)
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: SMALL MODULAR REACTORS FOR CLEAN AND LOW-CARBON ENERGY India PIB 04 Feb 2026 -- BARC has taken-up design, development and establishment of Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) suitable for deployment as captive power plants for the energy intensive sectors, repurposing of retiring fossil fuel based power plants, and deployment in remote location with no grid connectivity
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: POWER PRODUCTION FROM NUCLEAR ENERGY India PIB 04 Feb 2026 -- Presently, there are 24 nuclear power plants (excluding RAPS-1, which is under extended shutdown) in commercial operation in the country with a total capacity of 8,780 MW
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: MINING AND UTILISATION OF NUCLEAR ENERGY MINERALS IN RAJASTHAN India PIB 04 Feb 2026 -- Atomic Minerals Directorate of Exploration and Reserarch (AMDER), a constituent unit under Department of Atomic Energy (DAE) has discovered Uranium deposit in Sikar District, Rajasthan
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: NUCLEAR POWER GENERATION India PIB 04 Feb 2026 -- Nuclear power generation in the last two years 2023-24 and 2024-25 was 47971 Million Units and 56681 Million Units respectively.
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT ASSESSMENT India PIB 04 Feb 2026 -- A comprehensive Environment Impact Assessment (EIA) of setting up nuclear power plants at Gorakhpur in Haryana (Gorakhpur Anu Vidyut Pariyojana - GHAVP) including the impact on local water supply was carried out as a part of obtaining environmental clearance for the project from the MoEF&CC
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: COMPREHENSIVE SECURITY REVIEWS India PIB 04 Feb 2026 -- Considering the prevailing security scenario and threat perceptions, MHA has categorized DAE's vital installations into four categories as "A to D" on the basis of sensitivity
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: DOMESTIC NUCLEAR POWER GENERATION India PIB 04 Feb 2026 -- Nuclear power is a clean and environment friendly, base load source of electricity available 24x7, apart from having a huge potential to contribute the country's long term energy security
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: DAE EXPANSION PROJECTS India PIB 04 Feb 2026 -- At present, seventeen nuclear power reactors with a total capacity of 13100 MW are under implementation comprising of seven nuclear reactors under construction and ten rectors under pre-project activities

  • Ex-PM Harper: Canada should make 'any sacrifice necessary' to preserve independence against US Press TV 04 Feb 2026 -- Former Prime Minister Stephen Harper says Canada must be prepared to make "any sacrifice necessary" to defend its independence amid mounting threats driven by US President Donald Trump.

  • Shaping the future through partnerships: Foreign Minister Wadephul travels to the Indo-Pacific Germany Federal Foreign Office 04 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Wadephul visits five partner countries in Southeast Asia and the Pacific.

  • State Minister for Foreign Affairs HORII's attendance at the Critical Minerals Ministerial hosted by the Secretary of State of the United States (Outcome) Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 05 Feb 2026

  • Prime Minister of Armenia, President of Azerbaijan meet in Abu Dhabi Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia 04 Feb 2026 -- On February 4, the Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia, Nikol Pashinyan, met with the President of the Republic of Azerbaijan, Ilham Aliyev, in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates.
  • Human Rights: EU and OHCHR hold Strategic Consultations European External Action Service (EEAS) 04 Feb 2026 -- The European Union and the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR) held their fifth Strategic Consultations yesterday in Geneva.

  • Bishkek Hosts B5+1 Forum As Central Asia Eyes Stronger Economic Ties With US RFE/RL 04 Feb 2026 -- Bishkek is hosting the B5+1 Forum this week, bringing together officials and business leaders from Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Uzbekistan, and the United States to explore investment opportunities and deepen economic ties.

  • Colombian President Says Ready to Supply Electricity to Venezuela Sputnik 04 Feb 2026 -- Colombia is ready to start supplying electricity to Venezuela, all the necessary capabilities already exist, Colombian President Gustavo Petro stated after a meeting with US President Donald Trump.
